sections.label: Section 264
sections.original: μέθες · μίʼ οὖσα τῶν ἐμῶν Ἐρινύων
sections.translation: Let me go ! you are one of my Furies ,

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: μέσον μʼ ὀχμάζεις , ὡς βάλῃς ἐς Τάρταρον .
sections.translation: and are gripping me by the waist to hurl me into Tartarus !

sections.label: Section 266
sections.original: οἲ ʼγὼ τάλαινα , τίνʼ ἐπικουρίαν λάβω ,
sections.translation: Alas for me ! What aid can I find , when we have Heaven ’ s forces set against us ?

sections.label: Section 268
sections.original: δὸς τόξα μοι κερουλκά , δῶρα Λοξίου ,
sections.translation: Give me my horn-tipped bow , Apollo ’ s gift , with which he told me to ward off the goddesses ,

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: εἴ μʼ ἐκφοβοῖεν μανιάσιν λυσσήμασιν .
sections.translation: if ever they sought to scare me with wild transports of madness .

sections.label: Section 271
sections.original: βεβλήσεταί τις θεῶν βροτησίᾳ χερί ,
sections.translation: A mortal hand will wound one of them , unless she departs from my sight . Don ’ t you hear me ? Don ’ t you see the feathered arrows springing out from my far-shooting bow ? What !

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: τί δῆτα μέλλετʼ ; ἐξακρίζετʼ αἰθέρα
sections.translation: Do you linger still ? Mount the sky on your wings , and blame those oracles of Phoebus .

sections.label: Section 276b
sections.original: ἔα ·
sections.translation: Ah ! why am I raving , gasping ? Where , oh ! where have I leapt , from my couch ? Once more the storm is past , I see a calm .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: σύγγονε , τί κλαίεις κρᾶτα θεῖσʼ ἔσω πέπλων ;
sections.translation: Sister , why do you weep , your head wrapped in your robe ? I am ashamed that I should make you a partner in my sufferings and distress a maiden like you through my sickness . Do not waste away over my troubles ; for though you consented to it , yet I was the one that spilled

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: μητρῷον αἷμα · Λοξίᾳ δὲ μέμφομαι ,
sections.translation: our mother ’ s blood . I blame Loxias , for urging me on to do a deed most unholy , encouraging me with words but not in deed . I believe that , if I had asked my father to his face whether I must slay my mother ,

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: πολλὰς γενείου τοῦδʼ ἂν ἐκτεῖναι λιτὰς
sections.translation: he would have strongly entreated me , by this beard , never to plunge a sword into her throat , since he would not regain his life , and I , poor wretch , would accomplish such evil !

sections.label: Section 294
sections.original: καὶ νῦν ἀνακάλυπτʼ , ὦ κασιγνήτη , κάρα ,
sections.translation: And now , my sister , unveil your face

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: ἐκ δακρύων τʼ ἄπελθε , κεἰ μάλʼ ἀθλίως
sections.translation: and cease to weep , despite our misery . Whenever you see me give way to despair , it is for you to calm and soothe the terrors and distorted fancies of my brain . Whenever sorrow comes to you , I must be at your side and give you comforting advice ;

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: ἐπικουρίαι γὰρ αἵδε τοῖς φίλοις καλαί .
sections.translation: for to help our friends like this is noble .

sections.label: Section 301
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ τάλαινα , βᾶσα δωμάτων ἔσω
sections.translation: Go in the house now , my poor sister ; lie down and close your sleepless eyes ; take food and bathe your body . For if you leave me or fall sick from nursing me ,

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: κτήσῃ τινʼ , οἰχόμεσθα · σὲ γὰρ ἔχω μόνην
sections.translation: I am lost . You are my only ally ; I am deserted by all the rest , as you see .

sections.label: Section 307
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι · σὺν σοὶ καὶ θανεῖν αἱρήσομαι
sections.translation: I will not leave you ; with you I will choose to live and die ; for it is the same : if you die , what shall I , a woman , do ? How shall I escape alone ,

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: ἀνάδελφος ἀπάτωρ ἄφιλος ; εἰ δὲ σοὶ δοκεῖ ,
sections.translation: with no brother , or father , or friends ? Still , if you think it right , I must do your bidding . But lie down upon your couch , and do not pay too great heed to the terrors and alarm that scare you from your rest ; lie still upon your pallet . For even if you are not sick , but only think you are ,

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: κάματος βροτοῖσιν ἀπορία τε γίγνεται .
sections.translation: this brings weariness and perplexity to mortals . Exit Electra .

sections.label: Section 1.316
sections.original: αἰαῖ ,
sections.translation: Ah ! ah ! you goddesses swiftly careering on your wings , whose lot it is to hold a revel , not with Bacchic rites ,

sections.label: Section 1.320
sections.original: δάκρυσι καὶ γόοις ,
sections.translation: in tears and groans ; you black-skinned avenging spirits , that dart along the spacious air , exacting a penalty for blood , a penalty for murder , I beg you , I beg you !

sections.label: Section 1.325
sections.original: τὸν Ἀγαμέμνονος
sections.translation: Allow the son of Agamemnon to forget his wild whirling frenzy .

sections.label: Section 1.327
sections.original: μανιάδος φοιταλέου . φεῦ μόχθων ,
sections.translation: Alas for the toils which you , poor wretch , strove after to your ruin , when you heard the voice from the tripod , proclaimed by Phoebus ,

sections.label: Section 1.330
sections.original: ξάμενος ἀνὰ δάπεδον ,
sections.translation: at his sanctuary , where the hollows are called the navel of the earth .

sections.label: Section 1.332
sections.original: ἰὼ Ζεῦ ,
sections.translation: O Zeus ! What pity , what deadly struggle is here ,

sections.label: Section 1.335
sections.original: θοάζων σε τὸν μέλεον , ᾧ δάκρυα
sections.translation: hurrying you on , the wretch on whom some avenging fiend is heaping tears upon tears , bringing to the house your mother ’ s blood , which drives you raving mad ?

sections.label: Section 1.340
sections.original: ὁ μέγας ὄλβος οὐ μόνιμος ἐν βροτοῖς ·
sections.translation: Great prosperity is not secure among mortals . I lament , I lament ! But some divine power , shaking it to and fro like the sail of a swift ship , plunges it deep in the waves of grievous affliction , violent and deadly as the waves of the sea .

sections.label: Section 1.345
sections.original: τίνα γὰρ ἔτι πάρος οἶκον ἕτερον ἢ τὸν ἀπὸ
sections.translation: For what other family must I still revere , rather than the one from a divine marriage , from Tantalus .

sections.label: Section 348
sections.original: καὶ μὴν βασιλεὺς ὅδε δὴ στείχει ,
sections.translation: And see , a king draws near , lord Menelaus ; from his magnificence it is plain to see

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: δῆλος ὁρᾶσθαι
sections.translation: that he belongs to the blood of the Tantalids .

sections.label: Section 352
sections.original: ὦ χιλιόναυν στρατὸν ὁρμήσας
sections.translation: All hail ! you that set out with a thousand ships to Asia ’ s land ; good fortune is your friend ,

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: θεόθεν πράξας ἅπερ ηὔχου .
sections.translation: for you have accomplished , with divine aid , all that you prayed for .

sections.label: Section 356
sections.original: ὦ δῶμα , τῇ μέν σʼ ἡδέως προσδέρκομαι
sections.translation: O my home , some joy I feel to see you again on my return from Troy , but I also grieve at the sight ; for never have I seen another house more closely encircled by dire affliction .

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: Ἀγαμέμνονος μὲν γὰρ τύχας ἠπιστάμην
sections.translation: For I learned Agamemnon ’ s fate and the death he died at his wife ’ s hands , as I was trying to put in at Malea ; when the sailors ’ prophet , the truthful god Glaucus , Nereus ’ seer , brought the news to me from the waves ;

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: ὅς μοι τόδʼ εἶπεν ἐμφανῶς κατασταθείς ·
sections.translation: he stationed himself in full view and told me this : Menelaus , your brother lies dead , plunged in a fatal bath , the last his wife will ever give him . My sailors and I wept greatly at his words . When I arrived at Nauplia ,

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: ἤδη δάμαρτος ἐνθάδʼ ἐξορμωμένης ,
sections.translation: my wife already on the point of starting here , I was expecting to give a fond embrace to Orestes , Agamemnon ’ s son , and his mother , thinking that they were doing well , when I heard from a sailor the unholy murder of Tyndareus ’ child .

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: καὶ νῦν ὅπου ʼστὶν εἴπατʼ , ὦ νεάνιδες ,
sections.translation: And now tell me , young ladies , where to find the son of Agamemnon , who dared such evil . For he was a baby in Clytemnestra ’ s arms when I left my home to go to Troy , so that I would not recognize him if I saw him .

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: ὅδʼ εἴμʼ Ὀρέστης , Μενέλεως , ὃν ἱστορεῖς .
sections.translation: Menelaus , I am Orestes , whom you are asking about . I will of my own accord inform you of my sufferings . But as my first portion , I clasp your knees as a suppliant , giving you prayers from the mouth of one without the suppliant ’ s bough ; save me , for you have come at the crisis of my troubles .

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: ὦ θεοί , τί λεύσσω ; τίνα δέδορκα νερτέρων ;
sections.translation: O gods , what do I see ? What living corpse greets my sight ?

sections.label: Section 386
sections.original: εὖ γʼ εἶπας · οὐ γὰρ ζῶ κακοῖς , φάος δʼ ὁρῶ .
sections.translation: You are right ; I am dead through misery , though I still gaze upon the light .

sections.label: Section 387
sections.original: ὡς ἠγρίωσαι πλόκαμον αὐχμηρόν , τάλας .
sections.translation: How savage the look your unkempt hair gives you , poor wretch !

sections.label: Section 388
sections.original: οὐχ ἡ πρόσοψίς μʼ , ἀλλὰ τἄργʼ αἰκίζεται .
sections.translation: It is not my looks , but my deeds that torture me .

sections.label: Section 389
sections.original: δεινὸν δὲ λεύσσεις ὀμμάτων ξηραῖς κόραις .
sections.translation: Your tearless eyes glare dreadfully !

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: τὸ σῶμα φροῦδον · τὸ δʼ ὄνομʼ οὐ λέλοιπέ μοι .
sections.translation: My body is gone , though my name has not deserted me .

sections.label: Section 391
sections.original: ὦ παρὰ λόγον μοι σὴ φανεῖσʼ ἀμορφία .
sections.translation: Unsightly apparition , so different from what I expected !

sections.label: Section 392
sections.original: ὅδʼ εἰμὶ μητρὸς τῆς ταλαιπώρου φονεύς .
sections.translation: Here I am , the murderer of my wretched mother .

sections.label: Section 393
sections.original: ἤκουσα , φείδου δʼ · ὀλιγάκις λέγειν κακά .
sections.translation: I have heard , spare your words ; evils should be seldom spoken .

sections.label: Section 394
sections.original: φειδόμεθʼ · ὁ δαίμων δʼ ἐς ἐμὲ πλούσιος κακῶν .
sections.translation: I will be sparing ; but the deity is lavish of woe in my case .

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: τί χρῆμα πάσχεις ; τίς σʼ ἀπόλλυσιν νόσος ;
sections.translation: What ails you ? what is your deadly sickness ?

sections.label: Section 396
sections.original: ἡ σύνεσις , ὅτι σύνοιδα δείνʼ εἰργασμένος .
sections.translation: My conscience ; I know that I am guilty of a dreadful crime .

sections.label: Section 397
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; σοφόν τοι τὸ σαφές , οὐ τὸ μὴ σαφές .
sections.translation: What do you mean ? Wisdom is shown in clarity , not in obscurity .

sections.label: Section 398
sections.original: λύπη μάλιστά γʼ ἡ διαφθείρουσά με —
sections.translation: Grief especially has ruined me —

sections.label: Section 399
sections.original: δεινὴ γὰρ ἡ θεός , ἀλλʼ ὅμως ἰάσιμος .
sections.translation: Yes , she is a dreadful goddess , yet are there cures for her .

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: μανίαι τε , μητρὸς αἵματος τιμωρίαι .
sections.translation: And fits of madness , the vengeance of a mother ’ s blood .

sections.label: Section 401
sections.original: ἤρξω δὲ λύσσης πότε ; τίς ἡμέρα τότʼ ἦν ;
sections.translation: When did your madness begin ? Which day was it ?

sections.label: Section 402
sections.original: ἐν ᾗ τάλαιναν μητέρʼ ἐξώγκουν τάφῳ .
sections.translation: On the day I was heaping the mound over my poor mother ’ s grave .

sections.label: Section 403
sections.original: πότερα κατʼ οἴκους ἢ προσεδρεύων πυρᾷ ;
sections.translation: When you were in the house , or watching by the pyre ?

sections.label: Section 404
sections.original: νυκτὸς φυλάσσων ὀστέων ἀναίρεσιν .
sections.translation: As I was waiting by night to gather up her bones .

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: παρῆν τις ἄλλος , ὃς σὸν ὤρθευεν δέμας ;
sections.translation: Was any one else there , to help you rise ?

sections.label: Section 406
sections.original: Πυλάδης , ὁ συνδρῶν αἷμα καὶ μητρὸς φόνον .
sections.translation: Pylades who shared with me the bloody deed , my mother ’ s murder .
